extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter attacked 53km out after Mons-en-Pévèle cobbles, sustained 1:30 lead solo, won from two-up sprint against DILLIER
tactical_patterns: Sagan's attack succeeded at 53km (cobbled section 10 Mons-en-Pévèle), bridging to breakaway remnants before solo dominance.
Bora-Hansgrohe immediately shut down Teunissen-Gilbert-Politt trio, refusing dangerous early moves before Sagan's chosen moment.
Terpstra's chase group closed gap late but started organizing too late; 1:30 deficit never recovered despite quality pursuers.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Won while doing own bike repairs mid-race, suggesting mechanical resilience and technical self-sufficiency rare at this level.
rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: Only rider maintaining Sagan's cobble rhythm through final 20km; track cycling background underutilized in final velodrome sprint.
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: neutral: Led chase organization but podium from 1:30 deficit suggests passive tactical approach by chase group.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: negative: Dangerous early move (with Teunissen, Politt) immediately shut down, lacking support to sustain pre-Sagan acceleration.
rider_signals.PHINNEY Taylor: positive: American finished 8th, suggesting non-Belgian riders undervalued in cobbled classics handicapping despite Sagan's dominance.
